metadata.title:
C0mMENDS
THE MAYOR


and PRESS

CENTRAL LABOR COUNCIL


TAKES ACTION-STAND OF
THE PRESS INDORSED.

pages.raw_text:
For the purpose of bringing


about a modification of the city
ordinance prohibiting street speak
ing, over which the !. w. w. and
the police are at war, a committee
of five men was appointed at the
meeting Of the Central Labor coun.


Gil last night, to meet with the city


council, this evening.


A resolution commencing Mayor
N. S. Pratt for his promises to pro
tect the laboring men in the
clutches of the salary loan sharks
and to send The Spokane Press a
communication, thanking it for its
stand in the free speech fight and
against the money lenders, was
taken.


The resolution in commentator
of the act of Mayor Pratt follows:
Resolved, That the acts of May
or Pratt in protecting the laboring
men from the graft of the employ
ment agents and the salary loan
sharks. be hereby commended and
approved.


Delegates to the central labor
body explain today that their ac-
ton in the free speech matter was
not taken so much in favor of the
Industrial Workers of the World, as
it was for general principles. In
fact, labor unionists are free to say
that they are not in sympathy with
the methods employed by the l. w.
w. in forcing their point, but they
do believe in recognition of the
right to free speech.
